stimulus:
Welcome! In this study, you will play three short video games, completing a short survey after each game.

Please make sure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus for the duration of the study. Some games may use sound, so please ensure that your device's volume is not muted.

Press any key to begin.
Press any key to proceed to the first video game.

response: {"creativity-rating":2,"creativity-free-response":"It was kind of creative with the ability to drop lights to direct the character where to move, but it didn't really have anything else than that. The whole point of the game is to just move right, and it didn't involve any thought to proceed through the game.","originality-rating":1,"originality-free-response":"Nothing really felt original in the game. The whole game overall felt pretty generic. The enemies were not a threat, there were no real mechanics outside of dropping light sources for the character to move to and you just moved right the whole time.  It should've had more thought put into the enemies and their design and the way they interact with the player.","effectiveness-rating":1,"effectiveness-free-response":"The game was overall extremely boring due to how lacking in involvement it was. There was really nothing to it other than just clicking and making the character move right. The enemies didn't really matter at all and nothing else really stood out to make it enjoyable. ","authenticity-rating":1,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":1,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"It feels like they just made the game in an hour just to see if they could. It didn't really have much thought put into it, so I don't think it had any form of authenticity or effort. It was lacking in mechanics and unique enemies that interacted with the player."}

response: {"creativity-rating":3,"creativity-free-response":"It was sort of creative in the way that words interacted with the environment, but it was also too simple and at first fairly hard to understand how to make the words interact with things","originality-rating":4,"originality-free-response":"The ability to use words and drag them on objects to make the words affect them is pretty original. I don't think I've ever seen anything like that before in a video game. It was surprising to see that mechanic in the game and how unique it was.","effectiveness-rating":3,"effectiveness-free-response":"It was OK. It would've been more enjoyable if the game was longer and more complex. It was kind of frustrating figuring out how to get the words to interact though, I think a better tutorial could have been made for the game to show examples how it works.","authenticity-rating":4,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":4,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"The game feels authentic in being unique and trying to do something different compared to most games. response: {"creativity-rating":2,"creativity-free-response":"The game used the idea of being a paper plane and soaring through the wind as a way to show how free a person could be. At least I think that is what they were going for.  The reality of the game is it is just objects placed in a small level with mechanics that weren't thought out very much, as it's frustrating to control.","originality-rating":3,"originality-free-response":"The part that felt the most original to me was the way movement works in the game. Clicking and dragging to move with the distance of your mouse impacting movement speed was pretty original. The only surprise I had was that the objects in the level, which looked to be like saw blades didn't actually harm or interact with the paper plane at all. ","effectiveness-rating":1,"effectiveness-free-response":"It's interesting at first, but it quickly became frustrating when I had to overcome the first obstacle. Getting on top of the platform to get to the lighthouse was a chore because I couldn't accurately move the paper plane enough. It felt like it required pin-point precision which the games movement doesn't allow for. It mostly came down to trial and error.","authenticity-rating":2,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":1,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"I think the developer tried to do something authentic and unique, but along the way they just decided to give up and make a game with an unfinished mechanic such as the movement. The movement was lacking in detail as it was hard to be accurate with it. The objects in the game, like what I believe were saw blades didn't harm the paper plane or anything else. I also fell down one of the gaps and I didn't get punished or game over from it. Overall the game feels like it had ideas at the start and the developer gave up half way through and just released it as is."}
